Thorslund, B., Söderman, J., **Marsja**, E., & Selander, H. (Submitted). Developing Variations of a Driving Simulator Screening Test: A Step Toward Fairer Assessments and Safer Drivers.

Luccini, F., Holmer, E., Stenbäck, V., Ingo, E., Danielsson, H., Dahlström, Ö., & **Marsja**, E. (Submitted). Psychometric Properties and Cross-Group Equivalence of the Swedish Speech, Spatial, and Qualities of Hearing Questionnaire.

Larsson, S., Danielsson, H., **Marsja**, E., & Sjörs Dahlman, A. (Submitted). Feasibility of Using Virtual Reality When Investigating Road-Crossing Behavior in Older Pedestrians.

**Marsja**, E., Pernodd, J., Rodrigues, V., Mortazavi, A., & Blomkvist, J. (Submitted). Information Access for Older Passengers: A Mixed-Methods Study of Bus Travel With and Without Hearing Loss.

Ning, R., **Marsja**, E., & Holmer, E. (Submitted). Fluid Intelligence Predicts Speech-in-Noise Performance Under Cognitively Demanding Conditions Among Hearing-Aid Users.

**Marsja**, E., & Andin, J. (In Preparation). Temporal Relationships Between Episodic Memory and Hearing Loss.

**Marsja**, E., & Andin, J. (In Preparation). Long-Term Episodic Memory Trajectories Differ by Hearing Status and Hearing-Aid Use.

Fohlin, M., Åkerman, A.-K., & **Marsja**, E. (In Preparation). Exploring the Applicability of the Emotional Availability Scales in Dyads of Adolescents and Their Foster Care Parent.

**Marsja**, E., Brandén, M., Olsson, L., Granlund, M., & Danielsson, H. (In Preparation). The Effect of Having a Child With Disability and Personal Assistance on the Income of the Parents.

Blomkvist, J., Mortazavi, A., Rodrigues, V., & **Marsja**, E. (In Preparation). Public Transport for People With Hearing Impairments: A Literature Review.

Rönnberg, J., Signoret, C., & **Marsja**, E. (In Preparation). The Effects of Frequency of Hearing Loss on Recall of Speech in Noise.

Tirado, C., Holmer, E., & **Marsja**, E. (In Preparation). Cognitive Speed as a Potential Moderator of the Relationship Between Working Memory Capacity and Speech-in-Noise Performance in Hearing-Aided Individuals.

**Marsja**, E., Neely, G., & Ljungberg, K. J. (In Preparation). Deviance Distraction in the Auditory and Tactile Modalities After Repeated Exposure: Differential Aspects of Tactile and Auditory Deviants.
